amendments.body: Clear stale retry-attempt bookkeeping on terminal stage failures in src/contexts/workflow_composition/engine.rs:5825-5841 and src/contexts/workflow_composition/engine.rs:5897-5902. The new `retryable_failed_attempts` map records `will_retry=true` failures, but it is never cleared when the same stage later emits `will_retry=false` after exhausting retries or hitting a terminal failure. That makes `run resume` restart at the old retry count instead of attempt 1. Repro: `cargo run --quiet --features test-stub -- conformance run --filter SC-RESUME-005` now fails with `expected first resumed stage attempt=1, got 5` (see the scenario expectation in src/contexts/conformance_spec/scenarios.rs:4539-4642).
amendments.rationale: Manual resume after a terminal failure should get a fresh retry budget. The current logic can immediately burn through the remaining budget or skip expected attempt numbers on resume.

amendments.body: Do not treat the pre-backoff snapshot write as best-effort in src/contexts/workflow_composition/engine.rs:4622-4656. By that point the `stage_failed` event has already been durably appended at src/contexts/workflow_composition/engine.rs:4563-4600, so if `write_run_snapshot` fails and the process dies during the backoff sleep, the journal says the run failed/retrying while `run.json` stays in its old state. That reintroduces the exact 'non-resumable after crash during backoff' condition this snapshot is supposed to prevent.
amendments.rationale: Continuing past a failed durability write breaks the engine's normal snapshot/journal invariants and can strand the run on the next restart.

payload.summary: Reviewed the engine/process-backend changes and ran targeted checks, including `cargo test classify_exit_tests`, `cargo test resume_state_tests`, `cargo test process_backend`, and conformance scenarios `SC-RESUME-001` and `SC-RESUME-005`. I found one confirmed functional regression in resume attempt handling and one durability/robustness issue in the new pre-backoff snapshot path.
